description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a dark brown finish and a prominent grain texture. The head of the axe is made of metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end. The blade is silver-gray, sharp, and slightly beveled, while the poll is squared and dark, likely forged from steel. The handle appears ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip, tapering towards the end and thickening near the head for strength. The mounting of the blade to the handle is traditional, with the head fitted securely onto the handle, possibly wedged in place. No visible markings, engravings, or labels are present on the axe.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a metal wedge-shaped blade, featuring a dark finish and ergonomic grip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the curved wooden handle and the wedge-shaped metal blade.
description_view_1: Narrow edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: Rear edge-on view focusing on the back of the axe head and the handle's taper.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, and a smooth, dark-stained finish.
attribute_blade_design: Single wedge-shaped steel blade with a beveled edge and a flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade head fitted onto the handle.
